title: Start Here – IS3513
headerSubtitle: Complete these 4 checkpoints before starting any labs

sections.html: Complete these 4 checkpoints in order before diving into assignments. Each step ensures you’re set up for success.

sections.label: ✓ 1. Read the Syllabus
paragraphs: The syllabus is your contract for grading, policies, and expectations. It covers late work policy, drop policies, academic integrity, and everything else that governs this course.

sections.label: ✓ 2. Obtain the Textbook
paragraphs: Required: Conklin & White, Principles of Computer Security: CompTIA Security+ and Beyond, Sixth Edition (ISBN: 9781260474312). McGraw-Hill Education.
items: UTSA Bookstore — Physical or digital; may be included in course materials fee
McGraw-Hill Connect — Digital access; check Canvas for course-specific link
Amazon / other retailers — ISBN lookup for used copies; ensure 6th edition

sections.label: ✓ 3. Join Discord (Optional but Recommended)
paragraphs: Discord is the fastest way to get help in this course. Most questions are answered within minutes by classmates or the instructor. You must read the Terms of Service before joining.

sections.label: ✓ 4. Set Up Your Kali VM
paragraphs: Your Kali Linux VM is your security workstation for the entire semester. Every lab runs on it. Set it up now and name it correctly.
VM naming convention: kali-lastname-studentID (e.g., kali-newsom-abc123). This name must appear in every screenshot.

sections.label: What’s Next?
sections.html: After completing all 4 checkpoints, head to Module 1: Threat Intelligence & Reconnaissance to begin your NEXUS journey.
Questions? Check How to Get Help or post in Discord. Don’t wait until you’re stuck — ask early.
